channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The pandas are coming! The pandas are coming!
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The National Zoo's long dark panda drought seems to be coming to an end. Eleven months after the zoo said sent its three wildly popular pandas — Mei Xiang, Tian Tian and their cub Xiao Qi Ji — back to China, a new pair of bears is set to arrive.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FDA shakes up weight loss market as Mounjaro shortage ends
item.description: Federal regulators shook up the booming weight loss drug marketplace when they announced Mounjaro and Zepbound to be no longer in shortage after nearly two years.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WGN 9

item.title: Harris and Trump campaigning in battleground Pennsylvania on Monday
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump will take their fight for Pennsylvania to opposite ends of the state on Monday, with Harris speaking in the northwest corner in Erie and Trump in the southeastern suburbs of Philadelphia.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Republican lawsuits target rules for overseas voters, but those ballots are already sent
item.description: Trump and his Republican allies contend these ballots could be part of an elaborate scheme to steal the election from him, a claim for which there is no evidence. Their challenge comes as the voters who receive the ballots are increasingly from groups that are presumed to be Democratic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: FEMA temporarily pauses aid in parts of NC over reported threats
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) temporarily paused aid in parts of North Carolina this weekend after alleged threats targeted the agency’s personnel aiding in recovery efforts following Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KREX 5

item.title: Missing Texas mom, luxury real estate agent seen in last photo before disappearance as family fears worst
item.description: A new photo of missing Texas mom of four Suzanne Simpson has emerged, and her husband, who was arrested, is reportedly facing additional sealed charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why are scientists so interested in Europa?
item.description: NASA's Europa Clipper soared into space Monday, marking a major step toward exploring one of the most intriguing moons in our solar system.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Idaho prosecutors reject student murders suspect's longshot bid to have death penalty taken off table
item.description: Bryan Kohberger should face the death penalty if convicted of murder in deaths of four University of Idaho students, according to prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Group associated with polygamous sect builds fences on US Forest Service lands
item.description: A group in Colorado has fenced a parcel of U.S. Forest Service Land in an attempt to claim the land under the Homestead Act of 1862.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ruth Chepngetich of Kenya smashes world record by nearly 2 minutes at Chicago Marathon
item.description: Ruth Chepngetich of Kenya smashed the world record by nearly two minutes at the Chicago Marathon on Sunday, winning in 2:09:56.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Family trying to get ill Colorado dad home from European cruise
item.description: A Colorado family is desperately trying to get their dad from Englewood home after a sudden and severe illness in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Kerridge, NewsNation

item.title: Family desperately trying to get Colorado dad home after he fell ill on European cruise
item.description: A Colorado family is desperately trying to get their dad from Englewood home after a sudden and severe illness in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Kerridge, Fox 5

item.title: In an engineering feat, mechanical SpaceX arms catch Starship rocket booster back at the launch pad
item.description: SpaceX pulled off the boldest test flight yet of its enormous Starship rocket on Sunday, catching the returning booster back at the launch pad with mechanical arms. A jubilant Elon Musk called it “science fiction without the fiction part.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What you should do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: Phones hold so much of our digital lives — emails, social media and bank accounts, photos, chat messages and more — that if they ever get stolen or go missing, it can cause major disruption
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What is the purpose of an open-front toilet seat?
item.description: Open-front toilet seats — or “U-shaped” seats — are common in public bathrooms or commercial spaces, but they’re almost never the norm in private residences.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Video shows mechanical SpaceX arms catching Starship rocket booster back at the launch pad
item.description: SpaceX pulled off the boldest test flight yet of its enormous Starship rocket on Sunday, catching the returning booster back at the launch pad with mechanical arms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Will Donald Trump and Kamala Harris debate again?
item.description: While there was originally talk of more than one meeting between Trump and Harris, the former president made his intentions clear with a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Days left to claim cash in Oracle settlement that 'hundreds of millions' qualify for
item.description: The deadline is fast approaching for people to request a piece of the $115 million settlement fund Oracle agreed to pay out, settling claims it violated many people's privacy by selling their information to third parties.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Doritos to open its first-ever restaurant
item.description: It’s not just chips that will be available; the location will serve everything from margaritas and veggie dumplings to ice cream and California rolls, all with a Doritos twist.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, WGN 9

item.title: Texas middle school cheerleaders suffer first and second degree burns after 'punishment' from coach
item.description: Thirteen cheerleaders at Evans Middle School in Lubbock, Texas are suffering from first and second-degree burns on their hands after coach punishes team.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Washburn, Fox 5

item.title: UCLA professor says he’s homeless due to low pay
item.description: (KTLA) -- In a now-viral TikTok video, Dr. Daniel McKeown, a professor at UCLA, claims low pay has left him homeless, shocking many of his followers. "Hi everyone, my name is Daniel, and I’m an astrophysics professor at UCLA.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, KRON4

item.title: Visitor finds 2.3-carat diamond at Arkansas state park
item.description: A visitor to the Arkansas Crater of Diamonds State Park left with a 2.3-carat diamond to commemorate the trip.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, KRON4

item.title: With new rule changes, are real estate agents still necessary?
item.description: Some are deciding to sell their real estate without an agent and finding the process easier than expected.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: Florida deputies help pregnant woman in labor during Milton
item.description: Polk County Sheriff Grady Judd said that some of the office's deputies helped transport a pregnant woman who was going into labor to the hospital early Thursday morning, as Hurricane Milton brought widespread power outages, high winds and flooding to Central Florida.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, WGN 9

item.title: VIDEO: Dad gets dragged by car operated by 9-year-old son
item.description: A Toledo man was injured Sunday after he allegedly took his 9-year-old son on a driving lesson while drunk.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Venice Beach home buried in sand after Milton
item.description: While most people are dealing with water in their homes, some homeowners are battling an inundation of sand.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, WGN 9

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, clear debris after Hurricane Milton tore through Florida
item.description: LITHIA, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS O'MEARA, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY O'NEIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina governor blasts 'flat out lie' from Trump on disaster aid
item.description: "Trump’s lies and conspiracy theories have hurt the morale of first responders and people who lost everything, helped scam artists and put government and rescue workers in danger," said North Carolina Gov. Roy Cooper.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: NY prosecutors want to combine Harvey Weinstein’s criminal cases into a single trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Prosecutors are asking a Manhattan judge to consolidate the two sex crime cases that Harvey Weinstein faces in New York into a single trial this year — a move that the disgraced movie mogul’s lawyers oppose.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Catholic League accuses Whitmer of mocking Eucharist with Dorito
item.description: Podcaster Liz Plank posted a video on her Instagram showing herself being fed a Dorito by Whitmer, who's standing above Plank.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WGN 9

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, pick up debris after Milton
item.description: Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and tore homes to pieces, flooded streets and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 1 person dies and 12 are rescued after elevator malfunctions at Colorado gold mine tourist site
item.description: DENVER (AP) — One person was killed and 12 people were rescued after being trapped for about six hours at the bottom of a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Milton causes widespread power outages and several deaths in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton brought powerful winds, a dangerous storm surge and flooding to much of Florida after making landfall along the Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm. It weakened as it plowed through Florida late Wednesday into Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A former DEA agent is convicted of protecting drug traffickers
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — A former U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration agent in Buffalo, New York, was convicted of corruption Thursday following a second trial on charges he used his position to protect drug traffickers he believed were associated with organized crime.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Condemned inmate Richard Moore wants someone other than South Carolina's governor to decide clemency
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina inmate scheduled to be executed in just over three weeks is asking a federal judge to take away the power of granting clemency from the governor who is a former state attorney general and place it with a parole board.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane Helene: More than 90 reported dead in North Carolina, authorities can’t yet confirm how many missing
item.description: Authorities in North Carolina have confirmed 94 storm-related fatalities from Hurricane Helene but many still remain missing or unaccounted for.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A federal judge rejects a call to reopen voter registration in Georgia after Hurricane Helene
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A federal judge said Thursday that she won't order the presidential battleground state of Georgia to reopen voter registration for November’s elections despite recent disruptions to registration caused by Hurricane Helene. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4
